source.title: Dopamine in motivational control: rewarding, aversive, and alerting.

confidence_rationale: Authoritative review proposing that DA neurons split into VALUE-coding (reward-seeking, evaluation) vs SALIENCE-coding (orienting, cognition, general motivation) populations. Mixed verdict for VALENCE_EXCITEMENT: this framing supports the architectural separation principle (multiple anticipatory-affect channels are biologically real) but partially weakens the strong reading (excitement might be already captured by salience-coding rather than needing its own value-coded channel). Reduces but does not refute the 5th-channel proposal.
failure_signatures: Salience-coding DA neurons respond to BOTH appetitive and aversive salient cues -- they are not positive-valence-selective. If excitement maps onto this population, it cannot be a 'positive-valence-only 5th channel'.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Midbrain dopamine neurons come in multiple types: a value-coding population (reward-preferring, encodes motivational value, supports seeking and value learning) and a salience-coding population (responds to both rewarding and aversive cues, supports orienting / general arousal / cognition). An alerting signal augments both.
mapping.ree_translation: The architectural alternative for excitement is that it is the salience-coding signal, not a value-coding signal. Under that reading, REE could capture excitement via z_beta (which already modulates heartbeat / general arousal) rather than via a positive-valence-specific 5th channel in the residue field. The Knutson 2001a NAcc-anticipation signature, however, IS positive-valence-selective (no NAcc response to punishment anticipation), suggesting that the human-fMRI excitement signature is value-coded, not pure salience.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper is a primate / rodent neurophysiology review; the human-fMRI MID literature does not perfectly align with the value-vs-salience split (NAcc BOLD likely integrates both populations spatially). The mapping to REE depends on whether we adopt the value-channel framing (separate excitement) or the salience-modulator framing (shared with z_beta).
mapping.source_context: Review of primate and rodent single-unit electrophysiology of midbrain DA neurons; cross-species mechanistic synthesis.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Bromberg-Martin Neuron review is canonical). Mapping fidelity moderate because the value/salience split poses an architectural question rather than answering it -- the paper itself is consistent with both REE readings.
